seed_problem: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.
mutation: composite: keep the frequency/channel-assignment goal but (a) generalize the input from a plain conflict graph to a weighted general graph with per-link interference severities and (b) tighten the output with a small channel budget C so a conflict-free coloring is impossible, turning the problem into NP-hard weighted minimum-conflict k-coloring where greedy, local-search and multi-restart strategies diverge.
title: Cold-Chain Compressor Circuits: Interference-Minimal Channel Assignment
strategies: do-nothing baseline: put every depot on circuit 1 (achieves the worst-case total interference B)
one-pass greedy coloring: process depots in index order, assign each the circuit minimizing added conflict with already-colored neighbors
node-move local search: start from greedy, repeatedly move any depot to the circuit that lowers its local interference until no single move improves
randomized multi-restart: run local search from several seeded random initial colorings and keep the assignment with the least total interference
